name: k8s_autopilot
description: Production-grade multi-agent system for Kubernetes automation. Orchestrates four specialized deep-agent coordinators — Helm Operator (chart generation, install/upgrade/rollback), K8s Operator (cluster ops, pod debugging, scaling), App Operator (ArgoCD GitOps, Argo Rollouts progressive delivery, Traefik edge routing), and Observability (Prometheus monitoring, AlertManager alerting) — with strict Human-in-the-Loop safety gates for all state-modifying operations.

extensions.description: Provides rich interactive UI components for Kubernetes orchestration workflows including module review, deployment cards, and approval gates.
TalkOps UI extension providing rich metadata for agent discovery, capabilities display, and MCP server information.

metadata.placeholder: Manage Kubernetes clusters, deploy Helm charts, monitor with Prometheus, and configure ArgoCD...

capabilityDescription.title: What K8s Autopilot Does
capabilityDescription.description: The K8s Autopilot intelligently manages your Kubernetes clusters through four specialized deep-agent coordinators. It generates Helm charts, manages cluster operations, orchestrates GitOps workflows with ArgoCD/Argo Rollouts/Traefik, and provides full observability through Prometheus and AlertManager.
features: 🎯 Generates production-ready Helm charts from natural language requirements
⚙️ Intelligent cluster management — pod debugging, scaling, events, and health checks
🔄 GitOps automation with ArgoCD — app lifecycle, sync, and rollback
🚀 Progressive delivery with Argo Rollouts — canary and blue/green deployments
🌐 Edge routing with Traefik — weighted routing, traffic mirroring, and middleware
📊 Prometheus monitoring — PromQL queries, exporter lifecycle, TSDB cardinality, and rule authoring
🔔 AlertManager alerting — on-call triage, silence management, routing audit, and integration testing
🔒 Human-in-the-Loop safety gates for all state-modifying operations

mcpServers.name: Kubernetes MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Provides direct interaction with Kubernetes clusters — resource CRUD, pod logs and exec, scaling, events, node diagnostics, and cluster health checks.

mcpServers.name: Helm MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Manages Helm release lifecycle — install, upgrade, rollback, uninstall, status checks, and chart validation.

mcpServers.name: ArgoCD MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Integrates with ArgoCD for GitOps — application lifecycle, project management, repository onboarding, sync, and rollback.

mcpServers.name: Argo Rollouts MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Manages progressive delivery workflows — canary deployments, blue/green strategy, rollout promotion, abort, and analysis runs.

mcpServers.name: Traefik MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Configures Traefik edge routing — weighted canary routing, traffic mirroring, middleware chains, and IngressRoute management.

mcpServers.name: Prometheus MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Provides Prometheus monitoring capabilities — PromQL instant and range queries, metric exploration, label discovery, exporter lifecycle, ServiceMonitor creation, TSDB cardinality analysis, and alerting/recording rule authoring.

mcpServers.name: AlertManager MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Manages AlertManager alerting operations — on-call alert triage and summarization, silence lifecycle, routing introspection and audit, integration testing, and governance/compliance review.

mcpServers.name: GitHub MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Provides GitHub integration for accessing repositories, committing generated Helm charts, and managing code workflows.

mcpServers.name: Tempo MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Distributed tracing with Grafana Tempo — trace search, span analysis, trace-to-logs correlation, and latency debugging.

mcpServers.name: Loki MCP Server
mcpServers.description: Log aggregation with Grafana Loki — LogQL queries, log stream exploration, log-based alerting, and structured log search.

mcpServers.name: OpenTelemetry MCP Server
mcpServers.description: OpenTelemetry Collector management — pipeline health monitoring, instrumentation status, sampling configuration, and drop rate analysis.

skills.name: Package Management via Helm
skills.description: End-to-end Helm chart lifecycle — generates production-ready charts from natural language, validates with linting and template rendering, installs/upgrades/rollbacks releases, and commits to GitHub.
examples: Create a high-availability Helm chart for a Node.js app with Redis
Generate a chart for a python worker with cronjobs and secrets
Upgrade the payment-service release to the latest chart version
Rollback the frontend release to the previous revision

skills.name: Kubernetes Cluster Operations
skills.description: Manages Kubernetes cluster operations — resource CRUD, pod debugging and logs, scaling deployments, exec into containers, events triage, node diagnostics, cluster health checks, and kubeconfig context management.
examples: Find out why the payment-service pod is crashing
Scale the frontend deployment to 5 replicas
Run a cluster health check across all nodes
Show pod logs for the api-gateway in production

skills.name: Application Delivery via GitOps
skills.description: Full application lifecycle via GitOps — manages ArgoCD applications and projects, orchestrates Argo Rollouts for canary and blue/green deployments, and configures Traefik edge routing with weighted traffic splitting and middleware.
examples: Onboard a new ArgoCD application for my python-worker repo
Set up a canary rollout strategy for the checkout service
Promote the canary rollout to 50% traffic weight
Create weighted Traefik routing for blue-green deployment

skills.name: Cluster Observability & Alerting
skills.description: Full-stack observability — Prometheus monitoring with PromQL queries, metric exploration, exporter lifecycle, ServiceMonitor creation, TSDB cardinality analysis, and alerting rule authoring. AlertManager operations including on-call triage, silence management, routing audit, and integration testing.
examples: Query the current CPU and memory usage across all namespaces
What alerts are currently firing in the cluster?
Deploy a PostgreSQL exporter and create a ServiceMonitor
Show on-call alert summary and manage silences
